content:
type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: I'll own this file since both our changes touch image_to_base64/ImageBlock. Please send a DEFER+SPEC for normalize_image_bytes() so I can implement it alongside get_image_mimetype().

fields.note: I'll own this file since both our changes touch image_to_base64/ImageBlock. Please send a DEFER+SPEC for normalize_image_bytes() so I can implement it alongside get_image_mimetype().

content:
type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: Add a new method get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None on ImageBlock. Behavior: if self.image_mimetype is already set, return it immediately (do not re-guess). Otherwise, compute the mimetype by guessing from self.image bytes using the same logic currently inline in image_to_base64 (filetype.guess(self.image); use guess.mime if guess else None), cache the computed value into self.image_mimetype (so subsequent calls are O(1) and reuse the cached value), and return it. Note: image_to_base64's model_validator base64-encodes self.image in place, so if get_image_mimetype() is called after image_to_base64 has already run, self.image will be base64-encoded bytes rather than raw bytes -- that's fine, just reuse whatever guessing logic operates correctly given the current state of self.image (mirror existing behavior exactly, just factored into a callable method). Then update image_to_base64 (or your normalize_image_bytes refactor) to call self.get_image_mimetype() instead of inlining the filetype.guess(...) logic, so there is a single source of truth for mimetype resolution. No changes needed to resolve_image or other methods.
note: get_image_mimetype

Note: image_to_base64's model_validator base64-encodes self.image in place, so if get_image_mimetype() is called after image_to_base64 has already run, self.image will be base64-encoded bytes rather than raw bytes -- that's fine, just reuse whatever guessing logic operates correctly given the current state of self.image (mirror existing behavior exactly, just factored into a callable method). Then update image_to_base64 (or your normalize_image_bytes refactor) to call self.get_image_mimetype() instead of inlining the filetype.guess(...) logic, so there is a single source of truth for mimetype resolution. No changes needed to resolve_image or other methods.

content:
type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: Acknowledged: will add module-level normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] that detects already-base64-encoded input via base64.b64decode(image, validate=True) try/except, returning (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing), and will refactor image_to_base64 to use it plus call self.get_image_mimetype() for mimetype resolution using decoded_for_guessing.
note: owning types.py, implementing both normalize_image_bytes and get_image_mimetype

fields.note: owning types.py, implementing both normalize_image_bytes and get_image_mimetype

content:
type: DONE
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: We each work in separate isolated repo copies, so no literal clobbering occurred. In my copy: implemented normalize_image_bytes() (module-level helper, same signature/semantics as your spec) and get_image_mimetype() (caches into image_mimetype, lazily callable, correctly handles both raw and pre-base64-encoded self.image), wired into image_to_base64. Tests in tests/base/llms/test_types.py pass (12 passed). Submitting my own patch.txt now.
